content.text: When user selects a Component Thickness test (e.g., L4) from the Rules and Action test names list, system shall open a right-side configuration drawer Component Thickness test.
a configuration drawer to open when I select the Component Thickness test
I can review the rule configuration.
I am viewing the Rules and Action test names list,
the system opens a right-side configuration drawer titled “Component Thickness (L4)”.
System shall display a descriptive subtitle explaining the purpose of the test (e.g., Structural thickness verification.)
a descriptive subtitle explaining the test purpose
I understand what the test evaluates.
the system displays a descriptive subtitle explaining the purpose of the test.
System shall display the test scope badge (e.g., Local) in the header.
the test scope badge displayed in the header
I know whether the rule applies globally or locally.
the system shows the test scope badge (e.g., Local).
For Component Thickness (L4), system shall display Measured Parameters section.
I understand the evaluation inputs used for the test.
the Component Thickness (L4) configuration drawer is open,
the system displays a Measured Parameters section.
System shall display Original Thickness (t₀) and Measured Thickness (tₘ) as evaluation inputs.
Original Thickness and Measured Thickness displayed
I know the values used for thickness evaluation.
the Measured Parameters section is visible,
the system shows Original Thickness (t₀) and Measured Thickness (tₘ).
System shall display a descriptive text for Measured thickness section – “Thresholds are fixed under IRDS structural engineering framework. Manual configuration is not permitted.”
see a notice indicating thresholds are fixed
I understand that manual configuration is not allowed.
the Measured Parameters section is displayed,
“Thresholds are fixed under IRDS structural engineering framework. Manual configuration is not permitted.”

7.title: Display Severity Logic Based on Thickness Loss

content.text: System shall display Severity Logic based on calculated thickness loss percentage.
severity logic displayed based on thickness loss percentage
I understand how severity is determined.
the system displays severity conditions based on calculated thickness loss percentage.

8.title: Define Green Condition for Thickness Loss

content.text: System shall define Green condition for thickness loss as ≤ 5%.
the Green condition defined for thickness loss
I know when the component remains within safe tolerance.
thickness loss severity conditions are displayed,
it is defined as thickness loss ≤ 5%.

9.title: Define Amber Condition for Thickness Loss

content.text: System shall define Amber condition for thickness loss as > 5% and ≤ 10%.
the Amber condition defined for thickness loss
I know when moderate deterioration is detected.
thickness loss severity conditions are displayed,
it is defined as thickness loss > 5% and ≤ 10%.

10.title: Define Red Condition for Thickness Loss

content.text: System shall define Red condition for thickness loss as > 10%.
the Red condition defined for thickness loss
I know when the component is critically weakened.
thickness loss severity conditions are displayed,
it is defined as thickness loss > 10%.
System shall display Critical Escalation rule “if thickness loss exceeds 10% (beam-upright junction, baseplate anchor), immediate red flag escalation is required.
see the critical escalation rule displayed
I understand when immediate escalation is required.
the severity logic section is visible,
“if thickness loss exceeds 10% (beam-upright junction, baseplate anchor), immediate red flag escalation is required.”
System shall trigger red-flag escalation when critical threshold is exceeded.
the system to trigger a red-flag escalation when critical thresholds are exceeded
severe structural risks are immediately highlighted.
a thickness loss measurement exceeds the defined critical threshold,
the system evaluates the severity logic,
System shall not allow manual modification of severity thresholds for system-defined tests.
severity thresholds locked for system-defined tests
standardized engineering rules cannot be altered.
the Component Thickness test is system-defined,
the evaluation model modal is displayed,
the system prevents manual modification of severity thresholds.
System shall display Calculation Method section with the formula – Loss% = ((t₀ − tₘ) / t₀) × 100.
see the calculation method used for thickness loss
I understand how severity is derived.
the evaluation model modal is open,
System shall display the Close button in the evaluation model modal.
a Close button available in the evaluation model modal
I can exit the configuration view.
the evaluation model modal is open,
the system provides a Close button.

16.title: Allow Modal Dismissal Using Close Icon

content.text: System shall allow the user to dismiss the modal using the top-right close (X) icon.
dismiss the modal using the top-right close (X) icon
I can quickly exit the modal.
the evaluation model modal is open,
I click the close (X) icon in the header,

17.title: Return to Previous Screen After Modal Dismissal

content.text: System shall cancel the modal view and return the user to the previous screen when the modal is dismissed using Close or X action.
the system to return me to the previous screen after closing the modal
I can continue working in the Rules and Actions list.
the evaluation model modal is open,
I dismiss it using the Close button or close (X) icon,
the system cancels the modal view and returns to the previous screen.
